title: Strategic Planning Cycles
shortSummary: How to run quarterly planning that actually works — OKRs, resource allocation, retrospectives, and the discipline of saying no.
whyItMatters: Without a planning rhythm, founders oscillate between fire-fighting and dreaming. Quarterly planning gives you the structure to step back, assess, and redirect — before you waste another 90 days on the wrong things.
coreConcepts: Plan in 90-day cycles — long enough to achieve, short enough to adapt
OKRs (Objectives and Key Results) turn vague goals into measurable commitments
Resource allocation is where strategy becomes real — priorities without resources are wishes
A 'stop doing' list is the most valuable output of any planning session
